statement: Access control rules are written down, approved, and told to everyone. If you cannot point to the document that says how access works, you do not have
Every account has an approved request, named owner, defined access level, and current JML record.
All access is enforced through approved authentication and authorization controls. Local or static credentials cannot bypass them.
Users, administrators, services, and vendors receive only the access needed for their role. Privileged access is time-bound and recorded.
Failed login attempts are limited (≤10 before lockout), lockouts are enforced, and brute-force alerts are configured.
Remote access uses approved gateways, MFA for every connection, and session logging. Direct internet-exposed remote protocols are prohibited.
Mobile and BYOD access is allowed only when the device is enrolled, compliant, and managed.
Every system sends security, authentication, and administrative events to a centralized log platform. No system operates without logging.
Security logs are reviewed daily. Alerts are investigated and closed within SLA.
Audit logs are protected from modification or deletion. Admin changes to logging are also logged.
Audit logs retained 12 months hot, 7 years cold. You can retrieve a log from 13 months ago within 24 hours.
Every system configured to DISH baseline before production. Maintained against it.
All production changes authorized, documented, tested. No change without a ticket.
Configuration drift from the approved baseline is detected, flagged, and remediated.
Every hardware, software, cloud resource, and network device is recorded in an authoritative inventory.
Daily automated backups. Immutable storage. Annual restore test.
Recovery procedures documented and tested annually.
All interactive human access requires phishing-resistant MFA. Legacy auth disabled.
Minimum 12-character passwords. No periodic password changes (NIST says stop this). Banned password list enabled.
Non-human identities are managed with the same rigor as human accounts. Secrets stored in a vault.
Risks are documented in a risk register, scored, assigned to an owner, and treated.
All systems scanned for vulnerabilities on a defined schedule. Findings tracked to remediation.
Critical vulnerabilities patched within 7 days. High within 30 days. Deferred patches documented.
EDR on every endpoint and server. Automatic updates. Behavioral detection (not just signatures).
Critical system files monitored for unauthorized changes.
DMARC/SPF/DKIF configured. Anti-phishing tool deployed. Phishing simulations quarterly.
Every vendor with access to organizational data or systems is assessed for security risk before onboarding and annually thereafter.
Software and hardware acquired from third parties is assessed for supply chain integrity.
Networks segmented into security zones. Traffic between zones explicitly approved.
Sensitive data and admin traffic encrypted in transit. TLS 1.2+.
Sensitive data at rest is encrypted using approved methods. Disk encryption alone is insufficient for regulated data — it must be combined with column
Controls are assessed annually for design and operating effectiveness. Findings are tracked to closure.
All personnel complete role-appropriate security training before access and annually thereafter.
A documented incident response plan exists, is tested annually, and is accessible to all response team members.
Security incidents are detected through automated monitoring, triaged within defined timeframes, and tracked to closure.
Physical access to facilities housing sensitive systems is authorized, logged, and audited.
Security policies, standards, and procedures are maintained with named owners, review dates, and version control.
Security program metrics are defined, tracked, and reported to leadership on a regular cadence.
Personnel with access to sensitive systems or data undergo background screening before access is granted.
Security requirements are included in procurement contracts for all technology acquisitions.
Devices lock after 15 min inactivity. Re-auth required to unlock.
Sessions revoked on account termination. No persistent tokens.
Corporate wireless 802.1X. Guest isolated. Rogue AP detected.
Users cannot grant third-party app access without admin approval.
Every shared resource has a named owner. Ownerless locked after 90 days.
External sharing requires authentication. Anonymous Anyone links disabled.
Logging failure (disk full, SIEM offline) alerts within 4 hours.
Daily summary of critical events. Raw logs searchable.
All systems sync to authoritative NTP. Timestamps consistent.
Cross-org audit events logged and traceable.
Production systems can only be changed by authorized personnel. Changes reviewed before deployment.
Only authorized software can be installed. AppLocker or equivalent enforces this.
Configuration baseline exceptions have documented business justification, compensating controls, and expiration.
Critical data and processing capability maintained at an alternate location.
Contingency plan tested annually via tabletop and technical exercise.
Every user has a unique identifier. Shared accounts prohibited.
Identity verified before account creation. Remote proofing meets NIST IAL2.
Every system categorized by impact level (Low/Moderate/High) per FIPS 199.
Security alerts from all sources (SIEM, EDR, cloud, network) centralized and triaged.
Internet-facing systems protected against Denial-of-Service attacks.
Cryptographic keys managed through a secure KMS. Keys rotated annually. CMK preferred over platform-managed for regulated data.
Cameras, microphones, and screen sharing on company devices can be disabled by the user and cannot be enabled remotely without notification.
System maintenance performed by authorized personnel. Maintenance activities logged.
Remote maintenance sessions require MFA, are recorded, and terminated when complete.
Media containing sensitive data is sanitized before disposal or reuse.
Portable media (USB, external drives) encrypted. Auto-run disabled. Unencrypted media blocked.
Maintenance tools and diagnostic equipment are authorized and malware-free.
Re-authentication required for privilege escalation or accessing sensitive data.
Security alerts have defined SLAs. SLA compliance tracked and reported monthly.
Software verified for integrity before install. Signed binaries and checksums validated.
All input to applications and APIs validated. WAF blocks injection attacks.
Sessions managed securely: Secure+HttpOnly cookies, 15-min timeout, server-side invalidation on logout.
Personnel receive role-specific security training beyond general awareness.
IR team trained on roles. Annual tabletop + technical exercise.
All personnel know how to report incidents. Reports acknowledged within 1 hour.
Physical access to sensitive areas monitored. Video retained 90 days.
Authoritative SSPs exist for all in-scope systems. Current, accurate, reviewed annually.
Risk appetite, tolerance thresholds, and decision authority documented.
Security program documented with scope, governance, resources, maturity targets.
Software supply chain verified. SBOMs collected and scanned.
Actions attributable to a named individual. No shared accounts.
Critical communication paths have redundancy.
External users authenticate to same standard as employees. Guest MFA required.
Access revoked immediately on termination. Assets returned.
Open security findings are tracked in a POA&M with owners, target dates, and closure evidence.
Control effectiveness is monitored continuously, not just during annual assessments.
Third parties (contractors, vendors) with access to organizational data complete security awareness training.
Portable devices (laptops, tablets, phones) are encrypted, managed, and can be remotely wiped.
Insider threat indicators are monitored and investigated. Data exfiltration and unauthorized access patterns trigger alerts.
The contingency plan is documented, accessible to response teams, and distributed to stakeholders.
Access for customers or constituents (non-employees accessing portals, self-service apps) is authenticated and logged.
Cybersecurity insurance coverage is maintained, aligned with control implementation, and reported to underwriters.
The security program has a defined budget, aligned to control implementation priorities.
IR activities are coordinated with internal and external stakeholders. Communication follows a defined plan.
DNS and other name resolution services are secured against spoofing and cache poisoning.
Critical security functions (antivirus, firewall, IDS/IPS) are verified to be operating at defined intervals.
System architecture, network topology, and data flows are documented and current.
Personnel with privileged or sensitive access are screened before access is granted.
Systems being decommissioned follow a defined process: data sanitized, assets returned, access revoked.
